quranicRelevance: God as the All-Strong (al-Qawiyy), power/might in faith and physics, and desert travelers (Muqween - 'mata'an lil-muqween').

keyDerivations.meaning: Desert travelers, hungry ones, or dwellers in waste land

figurativeOrRareUsages: مَتَاعًا لِلْمُقْوِينَ (56:73): A provision/comfort for the desert travelers (who dwell in vacant/wild lands, quwwa).
discardedNoise: Mechanical and physical definitions of power.
Obscure details of desert tracking in empty lands.
summaryEn: The root represents strength and empty lands, capturing God's attribute as the All-Strong (al-Qawiyy) and comfort for desert travelers (Muqween).
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر القوة والقفر، معبراً عن صفة القوي لله سبحانه، ومنفعة النار للمسافرين والنازلين بالقفر (مُقوين).
rawText: أَقْوَتِ الدَّارُ 4 The house became empty, vacant, or unoccupied; (S, Mgh, Msb, K;) as also 🡻قَوِيَت. (S, K.) إِقْتَوَىَ 8 see 1. بِتُّ قَوِيًا , and قَاوِيًا, and مُقْوِيًا: see غَوًى and غَوِىٌّ in art. غو. تَقَوَّى 5 He strengthened himself; made himself, or constrained himself to be, strong; affected, or endeavoured to acquire, strength. See 1. === تَقَوَّى مِنَ الشَّىْءِ بِقَوْمِهِ: see تَمَنَّعَ. فُوَّةٌ Strength, power, potency, might, or force; contr. of ضَعْفٌ; (S, K;) in body, and in intellect: (TA:) vigour, robustness, or sturdiness; like شِدَّة. === قُوَّةٌ A strand; i. e., a single twist of a rope; a yarn; a distinct, and separately twisted, portion, of two or more which, being twisted together, compose the whole, of a rope, and of a string, or thread. See also ثِنْىٌ. === A faculty. Ex. السَّمْعُ قُوَّةٌ فِى الاُذُنِ بِهَا تُدْرِكُ الأَصْوَاتَ [السمع is a faculty in the ear by which it perceives sounds]. (TA in art. سمع.) === بِالقُوَّةِ Potentially, or virtually; as opposed to بِالفِعْلِ, i. e. actually. === قُوَّةُ لَفْظٍ: see لَيْتَ. قَاوَاهُ 3 He vied with him, strove to surpass him, or contended with him for superiority, (S, K,) in strength: (TA:) i. q. شَادَّهُ. (A, L in art. شد.) قَوِىَ 1 and 🡻تَقَوَّى (S, K) and 🡻اِقْتَوَى (K) He became strong: (S, K:) vigorous, robust, or sturdy; like اِشْتَدَّ, or the last signifies he became excellent in strength. (TA.) === قَوِىَ عَلَيْهِ He had strength, or power, sufficient for it; or he had strength, or power, to endure it; he prevailed against it; namely, travel, adversity, &c. And He or it, prevailed over him, or it. === See 4. === قَوِىَ is [said to be] originally قَوِوَ, because it is from القُوَّةُ: (I'Ak, p. 368:) but Lth holds قُوَّةٌ to be [anomalous,] originally قُويَةٌ. (TA.) I prefer the former opinion, and think it should be mentioned in art. قو, or I would rather head this art. قو or قوى, like غو or غوى. ISd holds قُوَّةٌ to be from قوو, like ثُوَّةٌ, q. v. from ثوو. قَوَّاهُ 2 : see شَدَّهُ. === قَوَّانِى عَلَيْهِ: see 2 in art. طوق.
